data.title: Redcare Pharmacy: Strong 1Q26 Outperformance Versus 2026 Growth Targets Supports Buy Rating
data.description:
Martin Comtesse, an analyst from Jefferies, maintained the Buy rating on Redcare Pharmacy. The associated price target remains the same with €150.00.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Martin Comtesse has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that indicate Redcare Pharmacy is outperforming its own medium‑term ambitions. German prescription revenues reached about €168 million in 1Q26, implying growth of roughly 55% year over year, which already exceeds the company’s full‑year 2026 growth pace implied by guidance.

Non‑prescription sales are also expanding at a healthy clip, with overall and German non‑Rx revenue rising around 10%, landing at the upper end of the 8–10% growth corridor targeted for FY26. Taken together, this strong start to the year, delivered despite previously cautious market sentiment following the March guidance update, supports the view that Redcare Pharmacy is executing well and merits a Buy rating.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: SPIE: Structural Growth, AI-Driven Demand, and Energy Transition Upside Create a Compelling Buy Opportunity
data.description:
Morgan Stanley analyst Remi Grenu upgraded the rating on SPIE SA to a Buy today, setting a price target of €51.00.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Remi Grenu has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to SPIE’s structural growth drivers and supportive end markets. He expects the company to benefit from increasing AI-related demand through its data center exposure, as well as from accelerating electrification and energy-efficiency investments that are relatively resilient to macroeconomic uncertainty.

He also highlights upside from a tighter energy market and future German fiscal stimulus, which should bolster organic growth and support SPIE’s mid-term margin ambitions. Despite short-term weakness from adverse weather and Middle East disruptions, he believes these effects are temporary, and sees the recent share-price pullback as creating an attractive valuation entry point for investors.

According to TipRanks, Grenu is a 2-star analyst with an average return of 0.1% and a 49.57% success rate.

In another report released on April 2, TipRanks – PerPlexity also reiterated a Buy rating on the stock with a €47.00 price target.
